title: Rediscovery of a Lost Morecambe and Wise Episode: A Cultural Artifact from the Permissive Era
summary: A previously missing episode of the legendary British comedy duo Morecambe and Wise, originally aired on September 16, 1968, has been unearthed from a private collection. The episode is part of their return to the BBC after a period with ATV, motivated in part by the opportunity to produce in color. The recording, preserved by a now-deceased industry professional, is in black and white but is deemed restorable by the charitable trust Film is Fabulous! (FIF). Professor Justin Smith, a cinema and television historian at De Montfort University and chair of FIF, characterizes the episode as fast-paced and witty, replete with trademark sketches such as a balloon dancer and a nudist camp scene that satirize the 'permissive society' of the 1960s. Smith emphasizes that the duo were in their prime, exhibiting the comedic spark that defined their legacy. The BBC intends to broadcast the episode in the coming months, offering a glimpse into a pivotal moment in British television history. This discovery underscores the ongoing efforts to recover lost media and highlights the cultural significance of Morecambe and Wise's work in reflecting and shaping societal norms.

background_read: Morecambe and Wise, comprising Eric Morecambe and Ernie Wise, were a cornerstone of British comedy from the 1960s to 1980s. Their BBC shows, especially Christmas specials, attracted massive audiences. The 1960s permissive society marked a shift in social attitudes, which comedy often satirized. Lost media recovery is a niche but important field, with organizations like FIF dedicated to finding and restoring old broadcasts.
Article_Structure: Main Points: A lost 1968 Morecambe and Wise episode was discovered in a private collection, preserved by a deceased industry professional, and will be broadcast by the BBC. Purpose: To report the rediscovery and its cultural significance. Evidence Evaluation: The article relies on Professor Justin Smith, a credible academic, and FIF, a reputable trust. Author Credibility: The BBC is a trusted news organization. Methodology: The episode was tracked down by FIF through private collectors. Critical Assessment: The article provides a balanced view, noting the episode's historical value and the technical challenges of restoration.

perspectives.description: View the episode as a valuable primary source for understanding 1960s comedy and society.
Anticipate the broadcast with nostalgia and excitement.
